page: Microsoft Azure E16ps_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the EPSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage capabilities for persistent disk operations.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across most multi-core and memory-bound workloads. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 28894.58 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9647.0. Memory subsystem performance is characterized by a read-only small-block cached bandwidth of 1127.15 GB/sec and an uncached large-block read bandwidth of 127.65 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1116.0 and a single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 8.81 GB/sec. Cryptographic performance is also limited, with a PassMark CPU encryption score of 2606.46, which falls into the bottom 25% of benchmarks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16ps_v5 leverages the ARM64 architecture to deliver high memory density without the overhead of x86 licensing costs. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require intensive single-threaded compute or advanced vector instructions like AVX. It is suited for database operations, as demonstrated by a PassMark database score of 10911.19 and a Redis SET throughput of 22.74 million operations per second. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, multi-threaded text processing, and software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure E16ps_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the EPSv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is equipped with 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, though it lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. Benchmark results show average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 28894.58 and a Redis SET throughput of 22.74 million operations per second. However, single-core capabilities and cryptographic operations are relatively weak. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive tasks, making it suitable for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 128 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16ps_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 16 dedicated Ampere cores and 128 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine delivering high memory density and dedicated physical cores for memory-intensive database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core
Lacks local storage, utilizing remote Premium Storage for disk operations
Delivers 22.74 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation
Exhibits lower performance in single-threaded and cryptographic tasks
